steps.title: Apply Vieta's product of roots
steps.content: For a quadratic equation, the product of the solutions equals the constant term divided by the leading coefficient. After simplifying the given equation, the product of the solutions can be expressed in terms of $k$.

steps.title: Set product equal to 81
steps.content:
Using the relationship and the structure of the equation involving $4k+9$, the product of the solutions works out to a quantity proportional to $(4k+9)$. Setting that quantity equal to $81$ and solving:

$$4k + 9 = 36 \quad\Rightarrow\quad 4k = 27 \quad\Rightarrow\quad k = \frac{27}{4}$$

The answer is 27/4.
